Projects
  Multi-Instance SaaS App with KubePlus & GKE  | BitBucket                                                                                July 2025
   Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE), KubePlus, Helm, Bash CLI, REST, NetworkPolicy
•	Utilized KubePlus to register Helm chart as a Kubernetes-native Custom Resource (CRD), enabling kubectl-based deployment of multiple tenant-isolated namespaces with network policies and dynamic resource provisioning.
•	• Extended app logic with a /metrics endpoint to track usage stats for each HTTP route; built kubectl-appmetrics-show CLI plugin to extract live instance-level telemetry via REST over custom kubeconfig.
•	• Packaged Helm chart with versioned .tgz, deployed production-grade containers to GCR for GKE cluster consumption, and parameterized tenant configs (e.g., GREETING, NODE_PORT, IMAGE) for scalable instance rollout.        
Helm Chart Deployment & CLI Tooling – “Greetings” Microservice | BitBucket                                                   July 2025
   Helm, Kubernetes (Minikube), RBAC, Bash CLI, YAML, REST APIs
•	Engineered a reusable Helm chart to deploy a containerized Python microservice across multiple namespaces (greetings1, greetings2), using dynamic Helm variables for replicas, ports, and environment configs.
•	• Enhanced application endpoints (/greetings, /listcontents, /getk8sobjects) with namespace-aware RBAC queries; passed current namespace via environment variable using Helm’s .Release.Namespace.
•	• Built custom kubectl CLI plugins (greetings-show, greetings-delete) in Bash to automate service introspection and release deletion via REST and Helm API calls.         
Kubernetes App Orchestration– “Greetings” Service | BitBucket                                                                            June 2025
  Kubernetes (Minikube, kubectl), YAML, RBAC, ConfigMaps
•	Deployed a containerized Python app from Docker to a local Kubernetes cluster using Minikube, implementing deployment specs with 3-replica pods, environment variables, and volume mounts.
•	Defined and configured Kubernetes Service (NodePort), ServiceAccount, ConfigMap, and RBAC (Roles/RoleBindings) using greetings.yaml, applying correct selectors and labels for resource management.
•	Connected local Docker CLI to Minikube’s Docker daemon to build and push custom images; validated app behavior using kubectl and curl commands for testing pods, services, and mounted volumes.      
Docker Web App Deployment – “Greetings” Service | BitBucket                                                                            June 2025
  Docker, Shell Scripting, Python (Flask)
•	Built and containerized a Python web service with Docker using a custom Ubuntu 22.04 base image, environment variables, and volume mounting to support runtime configuration.
•	Engineered RESTful endpoints (/greetings, /listcontents) to return environment-based greetings and host volume contents dynamically; exposed app via port 5001.
•	Scripted Docker image build and run automation using Bash (build.sh, updated-run.sh), enabling flexible runtime behavior through parameterized execution.     
Collatz Problem Solver with Optimization| GitLab                                                                                                   June 2025
   Python, GitLab CI, cProfile, Unit Testing, Optimization
•	Implemented the 3n+1 Collatz Conjecture in Python with memoization caching for performance optimization, achieving 100% test coverage and successful GitLab CI integration.
•	Developed and ran unit tests and acceptance tests (100-200 lines) to cover edge cases, achieving robust code reliability and debugging with cProfile.
•	Collaborated efficiently with a partner, making regular commits, managing 15+ GitLab issues, and submitting optimized code to Sphere Online Judge after resolving performance bottlenecks.   
TXFishSpots Web App IDB (Coddle)| GitLab                                                                                                          June 2025
  Flask, React, SQLAlchemy, MySQL, Postman, GCP, Bootstrap, Python, JavaScript, GitLab CI
•	• Designed and implemented a dynamic web application emulating IMDB to track Texas Fish Spots with three interlinked models using Flask, SQLAlchemy, and React.
•	Developed the front-end using React, creating the Home and About Me pages, integrating Flipcards with CSS for interactive design, and building a Developer Tools carousel using a wrap-around array. Also made a 30-second video using CapCut for user experience.
•	Collaborated on GitLab, using version control, handling 15+ issues, creating unit tests, and contributing to a detailed technical report and presentation.      
  Snake Arcade Game | GitHub                                                                                                                              January 2023

•	Utilized JavaScript classes to engineer the 1976 arcade snake game; pioneered a snake class that eats food and grows snake body with array shifting/traversal algorithm
•	Used scale algorithm to update resolutions correspondingly
•	Organized technical workshop to administrate front-end engineering concepts to 10 undergraduates.
  Image to ASCII Filter | GitHub                                                                                                                        November 2022
  HTML/CSS/JavaScript, P5.js Libraries
•	Engineered a live processes video converting each pixel to ASCII text corresponding to each individual pixel’s brightness value; calculated brightness values from average of RGB values
•	Utilized P5.js Libraries to create a multidimensional array-traversal algorithm to map pixels to black and white characters
•	Organized technical workshop to administrate front-end engineering concepts to 15 undergraduates

•	Organized playlists on average of 30 to 100 songs and converted to Spotify
•	Analyzed video data from the Liked Videos playlist of a YouTube account using the YouTube Data API to determine the contents of the HTTP request

Software Engineering | University of Texas at Austin	     June 2025 – July 2025
CS Upper-Division Elective
•	Developed full-stack web applications using Flask, PostgreSQL, SQLAlchemy, and deployed them to the cloud via Google Cloud Platform (GCP) and Namecheap.
•	Built and tested Python modules using unit testing, code coverage, and continuous integration with GitLab CI and Makefiles.
•	Collaborated on 2 major software projects, each emphasizing different aspects of software engineering: testing, cloud deployment, database integration, and RESTful APIs.
•	Learned modern development workflows, including version control with Git/GitLab, code documentation with pydoc/pdoc, and pair programming techniques.
•	Explored agile methodologies through weekly readings from Extreme Programming Installed and authored technical papers on topics such as team collaboration, CI, and interviewing strategies.
•	Practiced API design and documentation using Postman, and implemented secure client-server communication protocols.
•	Gained hands-on experience with React, HTML/CSS/JS, and frontend-backend integration for dynamic web applications.
•	Authored 7 programming assignments and a technical report, refining academic writing skills in line with the course’s Writing Flag designation.
•	Designed and normalized relational databases with UML diagrams, ER diagrams, and practiced SQL joins, queries, and schema mapping.
•	Demonstrated technical work during the final weeks, demonstrating knowledge of the software development lifecycle from conception to deployment. 
Cloud Native Computing | University of Texas at Austin	             June 2025 – July 2025 CS Upper-Division Elective
•	• Deployed containerized applications using Docker, authoring Dockerfiles, managing volumes, and following industry-standard container best practices.
•	• Orchestrated microservices and workloads with Kubernetes, working with Minikube, kubectl, YAML configurations, StatefulSets, ConfigMaps, and RoleBindings.
•	• Automated Kubernetes application deployments with Helm, creating and customizing Helm charts for real-world use cases.
•	• Implemented multi-tenancy and SaaS application patterns using KubePlus, exploring namespace isolation, RBAC, and cloud-native operator design.
•	• Monitored Kubernetes clusters using Prometheus, configuring observability stacks for metric collection, alerting, and performance tracking.
•	• Completed 4 major programming assignments and 3 quizzes covering cloud-native architecture, container orchestration, CI/CD, and logging/monitoring practices.
•	• Gained practical knowledge of CNCF tools and contributed to class discussions on open-source workflows and Kubernetes community contributions.
